meta.title: An Orthodox Priest Becomes an Evangelical Pastor
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund (Truth Unites)

questions.question: What does Gavin Ortlund say "irenic" means in his channel introduction?
Which denomination does Joshua Shuping serve as a pastor in this interview?
Where is Shuping’s church located at the time of the conversation?
What kind of church first evangelized Shuping after the bookstore conversation?
Shuping describes his pre-conversion spirituality with which label?
Which Orthodox seminary did Shuping attend?
options: Holy Cross Greek Orthodox School of Theology

questions.question: In which jurisdiction was Shuping ordained as a priest (as he states)?
About how long did Shuping say he served as an Orthodox priest?
Which author does Shuping mention as teaching universalism (all ultimately saved)?
What intellectual current does Shuping associate with Frithjof Schuon?
Which patristic figure does Shuping cite as early homiletic material that sounded like penal substitution to him?
Which Gospel book commentator does Shuping credit with substitute/criminal imagery related to atonement?
How does Shuping summarize Cyril of Alexandria’s definition of God’s wrath (as he paraphrases it)?
options: God’s passionate mood swings toward sinners
God’s justice when applied destructively—not that God is filled with a passion of anger
The Holy Spirit’s grief in the soul
A purely metaphorical fiction with no biblical basis
questions.answer: God’s justice when applied destructively—not that God is filled with a passion of anger

questions.question: During COVID closures, what kind of sources did Shuping prioritize teaching from?
options: Older conciliar / long-accepted catechetical documents (e.g., Filaret, Dositheus, Mogila)
questions.answer: Older conciliar / long-accepted catechetical documents (e.g., Filaret, Dositheus, Mogila)

questions.question: Which council/confession does Shuping associate with Dositheus and Jerusalem, 1672?
What liturgical text read on the Sunday of Orthodoxy does Shuping discuss regarding anathemas?
Which doctrine does Shuping say the Synodikon anathematizes affirmers of (in his reading)?
options: The Filioque (Spirit proceeds from the Father and the Son)
questions.answer: The Filioque (Spirit proceeds from the Father and the Son)

questions.question: Which English-language Orthodox dogmatics textbook does Shuping mention alongside strict exclusivist statements?
options: Lossky’s The Mystical Theology of the Eastern Church

questions.question: What does Shuping call much of Eastern Orthodox “unity” despite strong rhetoric?
options: Paper unity—with real disunity at multiple levels
Numerical unity measured only by baptism counts
Unity defined solely by shared language in the liturgy
questions.answer: Paper unity—with real disunity at multiple levels

questions.question: Which third-century bishop does Shuping use to contrast simpler unity criteria with later maximalism?
What baseline does Shuping associate with evangelical unity across denominations (the “solas” and creeds)?
options: Subscription to every post-Reformation canon law code
Christ alone, grace alone, faith alone, Scripture alone, God’s glory alone—often Nicene or Apostles’ Creed
Mandatory episcopal ordination for every pastor
Agreement on a single global denominational government
questions.answer: Christ alone, grace alone, faith alone, Scripture alone, God’s glory alone—often Nicene or Apostles’ Creed

questions.question: In Shuping’s German-village thought experiment, what happens to the congregation after the priests visit Wittenberg?
options: They disband and stop attending church
The same people become Lutheran after being persuaded by the gospel controversy
They move en masse to Constantinople
questions.answer: The same people become Lutheran after being persuaded by the gospel controversy

questions.question: What office relation does Shuping affirm (following arguments he attributes to Ortlund): elder and bishop?
options: Two completely unrelated offices with no overlap
One office—presbyter and episcopos as the same office biblically
Bishops alone may baptize; elders may not
Only bishops may read Scripture publicly
questions.answer: One office—presbyter and episcopos as the same office biblically

questions.question: What does Shuping say is the majority doctrinal position in Protestant history regarding the Eucharist (among systematic sources)?
options: Pure memorialism with no real presence
Real presence—while typically rejecting transubstantiation’s account of the bread
Virtualism that denies any union with Christ at all
questions.answer: Real presence—while typically rejecting transubstantiation’s account of the bread

questions.question: Which image does Shuping attribute to Bullinger / the Second Helvetic Confession for Christ’s presence in the Supper?
options: The sun in heaven with its rays truly present on earth
questions.answer: The sun in heaven with its rays truly present on earth

questions.question: What does Shuping advise someone dissatisfied with their church to consider before converting traditions?
options: Immediately assume the tradition is false
Externalize all problems outward and avoid self-examination
See whether God is inviting deeper growth and service where they are—and backfill their own tradition’s history
Stop reading the Bible until a new tradition is chosen
questions.answer: See whether God is inviting deeper growth and service where they are—and backfill their own tradition’s history

questions.question: Which Ligonier teacher does Shuping recommend for an introductory church history DVD series?
options: Steven Lawson exclusively on the Puritans
Stephen Nichols only on Reformed scholasticism
